title: Adultery and Fornication
subtitle: A Vile and Evil Path the Quran Forbids Approaching

verses.arabic: وَلَا تَقْرَبُوا الزِّنَىٰ ۖ إِنَّهُ كَانَ فَاحِشَةً وَسَاءَ سَبِيلًا
verses.translation: And do not approach unlawful sexual intercourse. Indeed, it is ever an immorality and is evil as a way.

verses.arabic: الزَّانِيَةُ وَالزَّانِي فَاجْلِدُوا كُلَّ وَاحِدٍ مِّنْهُمَا مِائَةَ جَلْدَةٍ ۖ وَلَا تَأْخُذْكُم بِهِمَا رَأْفَةٌ فِي دِينِ اللَّهِ
verses.translation: The [unmarried] woman or [unmarried] man found guilty of sexual intercourse — lash each one of them with a hundred lashes, and do not be taken by pity for them in the religion of Allah.

verses.arabic: الزَّانِي لَا يَنكِحُ إِلَّا زَانِيَةً أَوْ مُشْرِكَةً وَالزَّانِيَةُ لَا يَنكِحُهَا إِلَّا زَانٍ أَوْ مُشْرِكٌ ۚ وَحُرِّمَ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
verses.translation: The fornicator does not marry except a [female] fornicator or polytheist, and none marries her except a fornicator or a polytheist, and that has been made unlawful to the believers.

verses.arabic: وَالَّذِينَ لَا يَدْعُونَ مَعَ اللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا آخَرَ وَلَا يَقْتُلُونَ النَّفْسَ الَّتِي حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ إِلَّا بِالْحَقِّ وَلَا يَزْنُونَ ۚ وَمَن يَفْعَلْ ذَٰلِكَ يَلْقَ أَثَامًا
verses.translation: And those who do not invoke with Allah another deity or kill the soul which Allah has forbidden [to be killed], except by right, and do not commit unlawful sexual intercourse. And whoever should do that will meet a penalty.

verses.arabic: يُضَاعَفْ لَهُ الْعَذَابُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَيَخْلُدْ فِيهِ مُهَانًا
verses.translation: Multiplied for him is the punishment on the Day of Resurrection, and he will abide therein humiliated.

brief_explanation: The Quran does not merely forbid zina — it commands believers not to even approach it, recognizing that this sin begins long before the act itself, through glances, speech, and seclusion. Allah describes it as a 'fahishah' (vile immorality) and a 'sa'a sabila' (evil path), marking it as morally reprehensible in both individual and social dimensions. The Quran goes further to declare that those who commit zina become spiritually akin to polytheists — linking sexual immorality to a corruption of the soul's orientation toward Allah. The very fact that Allah prescribes a public punishment without pity underlines the gravity with which He views this violation of His boundaries.
capital_punishment: The Quran prescribes 100 lashes for unmarried fornicators (24:2). For married individuals (muhsan) who commit adultery, the punishment according to the established Sunnah (agreed upon by all four madhabs) is stoning (rajm) to death. Both punishments require four eyewitnesses to the act of penetration, making conviction extremely difficult — the primary purpose of the hadd being deterrence and moral declaration. The Prophet (peace be upon him) established rajm through his practice, and the scholars are unanimous on this ruling despite it not appearing explicitly in the Quran as recited.
